description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a pale, off-white coloration and subtle grayish undertones. Its surface is uneven, featuring a mix of rounded and angular contours, with some shallow depressions and creases visible, especially on the upper side. The stone appears to have a natural, weathered texture, lacking any sharp edges or artificial modifications. There are faint, mottled patterns across its surface, suggesting mineral variation and exposure to the elements. The overall form is somewhat squat and broad, with one side slightly more indented, possibly forming a shallow basin. This stone would be commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as a decorative or functional barrier along sidewalks and pathways.
description: A pale, irregularly shaped stone with a rough, weathered surface and subtle grayish mottling. It features rounded contours and a shallow depression on one side.
description_view_0: The stone appears broad and squat, with a gently sloping top and subtle surface undulations.
description_view_1: This view highlights a deeper indentation or basin-like feature on the upper left, with more pronounced surface creases.
description_view_2: The shallow depression is most visible here, giving the stone a slightly concave upper surface.
description_view_3: The stone looks more angular and solid from this angle, with fewer visible depressions and a smoother surface.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and faint mineral veining, irregular and natural in appearance
attribute_weathering: Moderately weathered with a rough, matte surface and some shallow depressions
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or planted areas, often partially surrounded by grass or mulch

license_info.creator_display_name: Museo Nacional de Historia Natural de Chile
